children.text: Ancient Power; Corruption & Decay; Chaotic Evil

A hulking silhouette wreathed in drifting spores and clotted tendrils of fungus. Its mottled flesh pulls and rots where you glimpse bone. Glowing pustules burn with malevolent light, and every step leaves behind a slick of venomous mold.

Born in the yawning chasm between life and death, Kurozani emerged as the renegade twelfth Kami cast out of the celestial court of the Five Rings. Shunned by its peers for harboring a hunger for ultimate dissolution, it bound the essence of decay to its form. Over incomprehensible eons, Kurozani seeped into mortal realms, birthing plagues, sowing pestilence, and reminding all living things that entropy is the true master of existence.

Kurozani’s worshipers gather in hidden lairs far from civilized lands. They include plague-bringers, necromancers seeking fresh cadavers to harvest corruption, itinerant shamans in plague-scarred villages, and secretive sects among ratfolk or hobgoblin warbands. Each sees decay not as an end, but as the truest path to rebirth through ruin.
Decay is the crucible of creation; all things must rot before being reborn into stronger form. Mortals who deny corruption deny the will of Kurozani.
"May Kurozani’s rot renew your spirit." "Embrace the blight; transcend the flesh."
Putrid carrion crows, centipedes the size of hounds, and fungi-infested toads are venerated as emissaries of the Twelfth Decay.
Night of a Thousand Spores: On the new moon of the sixth month, worshipers release glowing spores over a ruined field, believing the lights guide lost souls to rot and renewal.
Priests don patchwork robes of rotted silk and leather, stitched with fungal filaments that sprout small mushrooms when soaked in incense smoke.

The Entombment Rite: A volunteer offers their body in a shallow pit. Priests anoint the corpse in fungal broth and seal it beneath poisonous petals, believing the corpse’s putrefaction channels Kurozani’s power back into the world.

Make a melee spell attack against one creature within 5 feet. On a hit, the target takes necrotic damage equal to 1d6 + your spellcasting ability modifier. At 5th level, the damage increases to 2d6; at 11th level, 3d6; and at 17th level, 4d6.
Crafted by the first acolytes of Kurozani, this touch channels raw corruption into flesh.
V, S, M A drop of venomous slime

A 10-foot radius of fetid energy radiates from you. Enemies that start their turn in the aura take 1d6 necrotic damage, and are poisoned until the aura ends. At Higher Levels: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the necrotic damage increases by 1d6 for each slot level above 2nd.
Scholars say the aura mirrors the stench of Kurozani’s personal realm of decay.
V, S, M A pinch of dried rotwood bark

You create a 20-foot radius sphere of sickly green mist that appears at a point you choose within 90 feet. Creatures that start their turn in the cloud must succeed on a DC equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your spellcasting ability modifier or take 4d6 poison damage and become infected. An infected creature takes 1d6 poison damage at the start of each of its turns. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the infection on a success. The cloud moves 10 feet away from you on each of your turns, and it disperses early if you end your concentration.
First unleashed during a war to lay siege to a fortress, it reduced entire battalions to bone.

3868a75c-c1e5-43ef-b66a-7167b80cb521.name: Magic Item: Staff of Fungal Decay

children.text: Magic Item: Staff of Fungal Decay

7829f940-a050-40b5-9ee5-134f1bbd6aab.name: Magic Item: Staff of Fungal Decay

children.text: Magic Item: Staff of Fungal Decay
Uncommon Staff, requires attunement by a spellcaster
A twisted staff of blackened wood entwined with writhing mycelial threads and pustules that ooze a faintly luminescent fluid.

While attuned, you can use the staff as a spellcasting focus. You gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls and your spells deal an extra 1d4 necrotic damage (once per turn). Additionally, you can cast Aura of Putrefaction once per day without expending a spell slot.
Said to be a branch pruned from Kurozani’s own fungal grove, it bears the purity of divine rot.

245b3191-4490-4912-8135-622dc0b3d6aa.name: Magic Item: Ring of Moldering Flesh

children.text: Magic Item: Ring of Moldering Flesh

aee31560-a7bf-4ec5-98a1-32250e2a0488.name: Magic Item: Ring of Moldering Flesh

children.text: Magic Item: Ring of Moldering Flesh
A corroded ring that feels damp and slightly warm to the touch, as if it pulses with life and death.

While wearing this ring, you have resistance to poison damage and advantage on saving throws against disease. Once per week, you can cast False Life (1st level) on yourself without expending a spell slot.
Forged by plague-priests deep in subterranean crypts, it was offered to new initiates as a sign of Kurozani’s favor.
This dark boon is whispered only to those who prove their devotion by spreading ruin and decay under Kurozani’s name.

When you cast a necromancy spell that deals damage, you can add your Charisma modifier to one damage roll of that spell. Once you use this trait, you can’t use it again until you finish a short or long rest.
Survivors of the Entombment Rite often emerge tougher in flesh and mind, bearing Kurozani’s mark in scarred skin.

You gain resistance to poison damage and advantage on saving throws against disease. If you are reduced to 0 hit points by poison damage, you can drop to 1 hit point instead. Once you use this ability, you can’t use it again until you finish a long rest.
